entries.definition:
نُسُك nusuk [coll. n.] 1 holy rites, rituals, acts of worship (6:162)
إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ my prayers, my acts of
worship, my living and my dying are for God, Lord of all Beings
2 sacrifice (2:196) فَمَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ مَرِيضًا أَوْ بِهِ أَذًى مِنْ رَأْسِهِ فَفِدْيَةٌ مِنْ صِيَامٍ أَوْ
صَدَقَةٍ أَوْ نُسُكٍ if any of you is ill, or has an ailment of the scalp, [and
thus is obliged to shave his head before the time], [he should
effect] a compensation of fasting, or almsgiving, or offering
sacrifice.

مَنْسَك mansak [n., pl. مَنَاسِك manāsik] act of worship, ritual;
place where sacrifices are to be offered (2:128) وَأَرِنَا مَنَاسِكَنَا وَتُبْ عَلَيْنَا
show us our religious duties and accept our repentance.
entries.definition_english:
نُسُك nusuk [coll. n.] 1 holy rites, rituals, acts of worship (6:162)
إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ my prayers, my acts of

صَدَقَةٍ أَوْ نُسُكٍ if any of you is ill, or has an ailment of the scalp, [and

effect] a compensation of fasting, or almsgiving, or offering

مَنْسَك mansak [n., pl. مَنَاسِك manāsik] act of worship, ritual;

show us our religious duties and accept our repentance.

entries.definition:
n–s–w women, woman-like, pertaining to women; sciatica. In
some works the roots ن/س/و n–s–w and ن/س/ي n–s–y (q.v.) are
classified together, suggesting, perhaps, another overlap between
these two roots and root ن/س/أ n–s–ʾ (q.v.). Of this root, two
forms occur 59 times in the Qur’an: نِسْوَة niswatun twice and نِسَاء
nisāʾ 57 times.

نِسْوَة niswatun [pl. of paucity, no sing.] some women, a few
women (12:30) وَقَالَ نِسْوَةٌ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ some women of the city said.

نِسَاء nisāʾ [pl., no sing.] 1 women, womenfolk (3:42) وَاصْطَفَاكِ
عَلَى نِسَاءِ الْعَالَمِينَ He has chosen you above all women of the worlds
2 wives (4:129) وَلَنْ تَسْتَطِيعُوا أَنْ تَعْدِلُوا بَيْنَ النِّسَاءِ you will never be able

936
